forge --log-dir /path/to/logs/\n19\t//!\n20\t//! # Show version\n21\t//! forge --version\n22\t//! ```\n23\t\n24\tuse std::io::Write;\n25\tuse std::panic;\n26\tuse #[arg(long)]\n57\t    server: bool,\n58\t\n59\t    /// Server bind address (default: 127.0.0.1)\n60\t    #[arg(long, default_value = \"127.0.0.1\")]\n61\t    server_bind: String,\n62\t\n63\t    ///  /// Password for server authentication\n76\t    #[arg(long, value_name = \"PASSWORD\")]\n77\t    password: Option<String>,\n78\t\n79\t    /// Subcommands\n80\t    #[command(subcommand)]\n81\t    command: Option<Commands>,\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n85\tenum Commands {\n86\t    /// Initialize FORGE configuration (first-time setup)\n87\t    Init {\n88\t        /// Run in non-interactive mode (for CI/automation)\n89\t        /// Auto-selects first available backend or uses FORGE_CHAT_BACKEND env var\n90\t        #[arg(long)]\n91\t        non_interactive: bool,\n92\t\n93\t        /// Force re-initialization even if config exists\n94\t        #[arg(long)]\n95\t        force: bool,\n96\t\n97\t        /// Re-run setup on existing config (backs up config first)\n98\t        /// Use this to change settings while preserving user data (logs, costs, status)\n99\t        #[arg(long, conflicts_with = \"force\")]\n100\t        reconfigure: bool,\n101\t\n102\t        /// Only detect available CLI tools and show results (dry run)\n103\t        /// Does not modify any files\n104\t        #[arg(long, conflicts_with_all = [\"force\", \"reconfigure\"])]\n105\t        detect_tools: bool,\n106\t    },\n107\t\n108\t    /// Update forge binary from a URL\n109\t    Update {\n110\t        /// URL to download the new binary from\n111\t        url: String,\n112\t\n113\t        /// Expected SHA256 checksum of the binary\n114\t        checksum: String,\n115\t\n116\t        /// Filename to save in staging (defaults to \"forge\")\n117\t        #[arg(short, long, default_value = \"forge\")]\n118\t        filename: String,\n119\t    },\n120\t\n121\t    /// Rollback to the previous version\n122\t    Rollback,\n123\t\n124\t    /// Clean the staging directory\n125\t    CleanStaging,\n126\t\n127\t    /// Manage workers\n128\t    Worker {\n129\t        #[command(subcommand)]\n130\t        action: WorkerAction,\n131\t    },\n132\t\n133\t    /// Validate FORGE configuration\n134\t    Validate {\n135\t        /// Show detailed validation results\n136\t        #[arg(long, short = 'v')]\n137\t        verbose: bool,\n138\t\n139\t        /// Attempt automatic fixes for issues\n140\t        #[arg(long)]\n141\t        fix: bool,\n142\t\n143\t        /// Skip chat backend connectivity test\n144\t        #[arg(long)]\n145\t        skip_backend_test: bool,\n146\t\n147\t        /// Output results as JSON\n148\t        #[arg(long)]\n149\t        json: bool,\n150\t    },\n151\t}\n152\t\n153\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n154\tenum WorkerAction {\n155\t    /// Pause a worker (stop claiming new tasks)\n156\t    Pause {\n157\t        /// Worker ID to pause, or \"all\" to pause all workers\n158\t        id: String,\n159\t    },\n160\t\n161\t    /// Resume a paused worker\n162\t    Resume {\n163\t        /// Worker ID to resume, or \"all\" to resume all workers\n164\t        id: String,\n165\t    },\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\tfn main() -> ExitCode {\n169\t    // CRITICAL: Check for rollback BEFORE parsing CLI or initializing anything\n170\t    // This must be the first thing we do to detect crashes from previous updates\n171\t    #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n172\t    {\n173\t        use forge_core::RollbackResult;\n174\t\n175\t        match forge_core::check_and_rollback() {\n176\t            RollbackResult::RolledBack {\n177\t                failed_version,\n178\t                restored_version,\n179\t            } => {\n180\t                let restored = restored_version\n181\t                    .map(|v| format!(\"v{}\", v))\n182\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"previous version\".to_string());\n183\t                eprintln!(\n184\t                    \"⚠️  Update to v{} failed on startup - rolled back to {}\",\n185\t                    failed_version, restored\n186\t                );\n187\t                eprintln!(\"❌ Update failed, rolled back to previous version\");\n188\t                eprintln!(\"Please check ~/.forge/logs/forge.log for error details\\n\");\n189\t                // Continue running with the rolled-back version\n190\t            }\n191\t            RollbackResult::Failed(err) => {\n192\t                eprintln!(\"❌ Critical: Rollback failed: {}\", err);\n193\t                eprintln!(\"You may need to manually restore from backup\\n\");\n194\t                // Continue anyway - better to try running than to abort\n195\t            }\n196\t            RollbackResult::NotNeeded => {\n197\t                // Normal startup, no crash detected\n198\t            }\n199\t        }\n200\t    }\n201\t\n202\t    // Mark that we're starting up (for crash detection on next run)\n203\t    #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n204\t    {\n205\t        if let Err(e) = forge_core::mark_startup_in_progress() {\n206\t            eprintln!(\"Warning: Failed to create startup marker: {}\", e);\n207\t            // Don't fail startup just because of this\n208\t        }\n209\t    }\n210\t\n211\t    let cli = Cli::parse();\n212\t\n213\t    // Initialize logging\n214\t    let _guard = match setup_logging(&cli) {\n215\t        Ok(guard) => guard,\n216\t        Err(e) => {\n217\t            eprintln!(\"Failed to initialize logging: {}\", e);\n218\t            return ExitCode::from(1);\n219\t        }\n220\t    };\n221\t\n222\t    // Check if this is a freshly exec'd process that needs to install itself\n223\t    #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n224\t    {\n225\t        match forge_core::check_and_perform_self_install() {\n226\t            Ok(Some(install_path)) => {\n227\t                eprintln!(\"✅ Update installed successfully to: {}\", install_path.display());\n228\t                eprintln!(\"🚀 Restarting FORGE with new version...\\n\");\n229\t                info!(\"Self-install completed to {:?}\", install_path);\n230\t                // Continue with normal startup\n231\t            }\n232\t            Ok(None) => {\n233\t                // Normal startup, not an auto-restart\n234\t            }\n235\t            Err(e) => {\n236\t                eprintln!(\"❌ Self-install failed: {}\", e);\n237\t                error!(\"Self-install error: {}\", e);\n238\t                return ExitCode::from(1);\n239\t            }\n240\t        }\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    #[cfg(not(feature = \"self-update\"))]\n244\t    {\n245\t        // No self-update feature, skip installation check\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    // Handle subcommands that don't require the TUI\n249\t    if let Some(command) = &cli.command {\n250\t        match command {\n251\t            Commands::Init {\n252\t                non_interactive,\n253\t                force,\n254\t                reconfigure,\n255\t                detect_tools,\n256\t            } => {\n257\t                return handle_init_command(*non_interactive, *force, *reconfigure, *detect_tools);\n258\t            }\n259\t            Commands::Worker { action } => {\n260\t                return handle_worker_action(action);\n261\t            }\n262\t            Commands::Validate {\n263\t                verbose,\n264\t                fix,\n265\t                skip_backend_test,\n266\t                json,\n267\t            } => {\n268\t                return handle_validate_command(*verbose, *fix, *skip_backend_test, *json);\n269\t            }\n270\t            _ => {\n271\t                // Other commands fall through to TUI startup\n272\t            }\n273\t        }\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    // Install panic hook to ensure terminal cleanup\n277\t    install_panic_hook();\n278\t\n279\t    // Check if onboarding is needed\n280\t    if needs_onboarding() {\n281\t        eprintln!(\"🚀 Welcome to FORGE!\");\n282\t        eprintln!(\"No configuration found. Running first-time setup...\\n\");\n283\t\n284\t        if let Err(e) = run_onboarding(false, None) {\n285\t            eprintln!(\"❌ Onboarding failed: {}\", e);\n286\t            eprintln!(\"You can manually create ~/.forge/config.yaml or try again.\");\n287\t            return ExitCode::from(1);\n288\t        }\n289\t\n290\t        eprintln!(\"\\n✅ Setup complete! Starting FORGE dashboard...\\n\");\n291\t    }\n292\t\n293\t    // Validate config file if it exists\n294\t    if !needs_onboarding()\n295\t        && let Err(e) = validate_config()\n296\t    {\n297\t        eprintln!(\"\\n{}\", e);\n298\t        return ExitCode::from(1);\n299\t    }\n300\t\n301\t    // Check for required dependencies\n302\t    if !forge_core::check_dependencies() {\n303\t        // check_dependencies already prints error messages\n304\t        return ExitCode::from(1);\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    info!(\"Starting FORGE dashboard\");\n308\t\n309\t    // Check if server mode is enabled\n310\t    if cli.server {\n311\t        return run_server_mode(cli.server_bind, cli.server_port);\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    // Check if client mode is enabled\n315\t    if let Some(server_url) = &cli.connect {\n316\t        return run_client_mode(\n317\t            server_url.clone(),\n318\t            cli.user.unwrap_or_else(|| \"viewer\".to_string()),\n319\t            cli.password.unwrap_or_else(|| \"viewer123\".to_string()),\n320\t        );\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    // Mark startup as successful (app initialized without crashing)\n324\t    #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n325\t    {\n326\t        if let Err(e) = forge_core::mark_startup_successful() {\n327\t            error!(\"Failed to mark startup as successful: {}\", e);\n328\t            // Don't fail just because of this\n329\t        }\n330\t    }\n331\t\n332\t    // Log terminal dimensions for debugging\n333\t    if let Ok((cols, rows)) = crossterm::terminal::size() {\n334\t        info!(\"Terminal size: {}x{} (columns x rows)\", cols, rows);\n335\t        eprintln!(\"Terminal size: {}x{} (columns x rows)\", cols, rows);\n336\t    } else {\n337\t        error!(\"Failed to get terminal size\");\n338\t    }\n339\t\n340\t    // Run the TUI application\n341\t    match run_app() {\n342\t        Ok(()) => {\n343\t            info!(\"FORGE dashboard exited normally\");\n344\t            ExitCode::SUCCESS\n345\t        }\n346\t        Err(e) => {\n347\t            error!(\"FORGE dashboard error: {}\", e);\n348\t            eprintln!(\"Error: {}\", e);\n349\t            ExitCode::from(1)\n350\t        }\n351\t    }\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t/// Install a panic hook that restores the terminal before printing the panic message.\n355\t///\n356\t/// This ensures that even if the application panics while in raw mode with the\n357\t/// alternate screen enabled, the terminal will be properly restored so the user\n358\t/// can see the panic message and continue using their terminal.\n359\tfn install_panic_hook() {\n360\t    let original_hook = panic::take_hook();\n361\t\n362\t    panic::set_hook(Box::new(move |panic_info| {\n363\t        // Attempt to restore terminal state\n364\t        let _ = restore_terminal();\n365\t\n366\t        // Call the original panic hook to print the panic message\n367\t        original_hook(panic_info);\n368\t    }));\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t/// Restore terminal to its normal state.\n372\t///\n373\t/// This function is called both on normal exit and during panic handling.\n374\tfn restore_terminal() -> std::io::Result<()> {\n375\t    let mut stdout = std::io::stdout();\n376\t\n377\t    // Disable raw mode first\n378\t    let _ = crossterm::terminal::disable_raw_mode();\n379\t\n380\t    // Leave alternate screen and disable mouse capture\n381\t    crossterm::execute!(\n382\t        stdout,\n383\t        crossterm::terminal::LeaveAlternateScreen,\n384\t        crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture\n385\t    )?;\n386\t\n387\t    // Show cursor\n388\t    crossterm::execute!(stdout, crossterm::cursor::Show)?;\n389\t\n390\t    // Flush to ensure all escape sequences are written\n391\t    stdout.flush()?;\n392\t\n393\t    Ok(())\n394\t}\n395\t\n396\t/// Set up logging based on CLI arguments.\n397\tfn setup_logging(cli: &Cli) -> forge_core::Result<LogGuard> {\n398\t    // Initialize logging with the specified log directory\n399\t    // Either --debug or -v/--verbose enables debug logging\n400\t    let debug = cli.debug || cli.verbose > 0;\n401\t    init_logging(cli.log_dir.clone(), debug)\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t/// Check if onboarding is needed (no config.yaml exists).\n405\tfn needs_onboarding() -> bool {\n406\t    let forge_dir = get_forge_dir();\n407\t    let config_path = forge_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n408\t    !config_path.exists()\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t/// Get the FORGE directory path (~/.forge).\n412\tfn get_forge_dir() -> std::path::PathBuf {\n413\t    dirs::home_dir()\n414\t        .expect(\"Could not determine home directory\")\n415\t        .join(\".forge\")\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t/// Run the onboarding flow.\n419\t///\n420\t/// # Arguments\n421\t///\n422\t/// * `non_interactive` - If true, suppress output and auto-select backend\n423\t/// * `preferred_backend` - Optional backend to use (from FORGE_CHAT_BACKEND env var)\n424\tfn run_onboarding(\n425\t    non_interactive: bool,\n426\t    preferred_backend: Option<&str>,\n427\t) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n428\t    info!(\n429\t        \"Starting onboarding flow (non_interactive={}, preferred_backend={:?})\",\n430\t        non_interactive, preferred_backend\n431\t    );\n432\t\n433\t    // Detect CLI tools with diagnostics\n434\t    if !non_interactive {\n435\t        eprintln!(\"🔍 Detecting available CLI tools...\");\n436\t    }\n437\t    let (tools, diagnostics) = detection::detect_cli_tools_with_diagnostics()?;\n438\t\n439\t    // Filter to only show tools that are ready to use\n440\t    let ready_tools: Vec<_> = tools.iter().filter(|t| t.is_ready()).collect();\n441\t\n442\t    if ready_tools.is_empty() {\n443\t        // Use the guidance module to show detailed instructions (both interactive and non-interactive)\n444\t        eprint!(\"{}\", guidance::generate_guidance(Some(&diagnostics)));\n445\t        return Err(\"No compatible CLI tools available\".into());\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    // Select the backend to use\n449\t    let selected_tool: detection::CliToolDetection = if let Some(backend) = preferred_backend {\n450\t        // Try to find the specified backend\n451\t        let normalized_backend = normalize_backend_name(backend);\n452\t        let found = tools.iter().find(|t| {\n453\t            let tool_name = normalize_backend_name(&t.name);\n454\t            tool_name == normalized_backend\n455\t        });\n456\t\n457\t        match found {\n458\t            Some(tool) => {\n459\t                if !tool.is_ready() {\n460\t                    eprintln!(\n461\t                        \"error: Backend '{}' is not ready: {}\",\n462\t                        backend,\n463\t                        tool.status_message()\n464\t                    );\n465\t                    if tool.status == detection::ToolStatus::MissingApiKey\n466\t                        && let Some(env_var) = &tool.api_key_env_var\n467\t                    {\n468\t                        eprintln!(\"Set the {} environment variable and try again\", env_var);\n469\t                    }\n470\t                    return Err(format!(\"Backend '{}' is not ready\", backend).into());\n471\t                }\n472\t                tool.clone()\n473\t            }\n474\t            None => {\n475\t                eprintln!(\"error: Backend '{}' not found\", backend);\n476\t                eprintln!(\n477\t                    \"Available backends: {}\",\n478\t                    tools\n479\t                        .iter()\n480\t                        .map(|t| t.name.as_str())\n481\t                        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n482\t                        .join(\", \")\n483\t                );\n484\t                return Err(format!(\"Backend '{}' not found\", backend).into());\n485\t            }\n486\t        }\n487\t    } else if non_interactive {\n488\t        // Auto-select the first ready tool in non-interactive mode\n489\t        match tools.iter().find(|t| t.is_ready()) {\n490\t            Some(tool) => tool.clone(),\n491\t            None => {\n492\t                // Tools exist but none are ready - show specific guidance\n493\t                let not_ready: Vec<_> = tools\n494\t                    .iter()\n495\t                    .filter(|t| !t.is_ready())\n496\t                    .map(|t| {\n497\t                        let (missing_api_key, incompatible_version, missing_feature) = match t.status {\n498\t                            detection::ToolStatus::MissingApiKey => (true, false, None),\n499\t                            detection::ToolStatus::IncompatibleVersion => {\n500\t                                // Extract missing feature from diagnostics\n501\t                                let feature = diagnostics\n502\t                                    .rejections\n503\t                                    .iter()\n504\t                                    .find(|r| r.tool_name == t.name || r.tool_name == t.name.replace(\"-code\", \"\"))\n505\t                                    .and_then(|r| match &r.reason {\n506\t                                        guidance::RejectionReason::IncompatibleVersion { missing_feature, .. } => {\n507\t                                            Some(missing_feature.clone())\n508\t                                        }\n509\t                                        _ => None,\n510\t                                    });\n511\t                                (false, true, feature)\n512\t                            }\n513\t                            _ => (false, false, None),\n514\t                        };\n515\t                        guidance::ToolFixInfo {\n516\t                            name: t.name.clone(),\n517\t                            version: t.version.clone(),\n518\t                            missing_api_key,\n519\t                            api_key_env_var: t.api_key_env_var.clone(),\n520\t                            incompatible_version,\n521\t                            missing_feature,\n522\t                        }\n523\t                    })\n524\t                    .collect();\n525\t\n526\t                if !not_ready.is_empty() {\n527\t                    eprint!(\"{}\", guidance::generate_not_ready_guidance(&not_ready));\n528\t                }\n529\t                return Err(\"No ready tools available\".into());\n530\t            }\n531\t        }\n532\t    } else {\n533\t        // Interactive mode: show the TUI wizard for selection\n534\t        match wizard::run_wizard(tools.clone()) {\n535\t            Ok(Some(tool)) => {\n536\t                // User selected a tool\n537\t                eprintln!(\n538\t                    \"\\n✨ Using: {} ({})\",\n539\t                    tool.name,\n540\t                    tool.binary_path.display()\n541\t                );\n542\t                tool\n543\t            }\n544\t            Ok(None) => {\n545\t                // User chose \"Manual Setup\" - skip auto-configuration\n546\t                eprintln!(\"\\n📝 Manual setup selected.\");\n547\t                eprintln!(\"   Create ~/.forge/config.yaml manually to configure FORGE.\");\n548\t                return Ok(());\n549\t            }\n550\t            Err(wizard::WizardError::UserCancelled) => {\n551\t                eprintln!(\"\\n👋 Setup cancelled.\");\n552\t                return Err(\"User cancelled setup\".into());\n553\t            }\n554\t            Err(wizard::WizardError::NoToolsAvailable) => {\n555\t                // This shouldn't happen since we already checked, but handle it\n556\t                eprint!(\"{}\", guidance::generate_guidance(Some(&diagnostics)));\n557\t                return Err(\"No compatible CLI tools available\".into());\n558\t            }\n559\t            Err(e) => {\n560\t                eprintln!(\"\\nerror: Wizard failed: {}\", e);\n561\t                return Err(format!(\"Wizard failed: {}\", e).into());\n562\t            }\n563\t        }\n564\t    };\n565\t\n566\t    if non_interactive {\n567\t        // Only print in non-interactive mode (wizard already prints in interactive mode)\n568\t        info!(\n569\t            \"Auto-selected: {} ({})\",\n570\t            selected_tool.name,\n571\t            selected_tool.binary_path.display()\n572\t        );\n573\t    }\n574\t\n575\t    // Create directory structure\n576\t    let forge_dir = get_forge_dir();\n577\t    if !non_interactive {\n578\t        eprintln!(\"\\n📁 Creating directory structure...\");\n579\t    }\n580\t    generator::create_directory_structure(&forge_dir)?;\n581\t\n582\t    // Generate config.yaml\n583\t    let config_path = forge_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n584\t    if !non_interactive {\n585\t        eprintln!(\"📝 Generating config.yaml...\");\n586\t    }\n587\t    generator::generate_config_yaml(&selected_tool, &config_path)?;\n588\t\n589\t    // Generate launcher script\n590\t    let launcher_name = format!(\"{}-launcher\", selected_tool.name);\n591\t    let launcher_path = forge_dir.join(\"launchers\").join(&launcher_name);\n592\t    if !non_interactive {\n593\t        eprintln!(\"🚀 Generating launcher script...\");\n594\t    }\n595\t    generator::generate_launcher_script(&selected_tool, &launcher_path)?;\n596\t\n597\t    if !non_interactive {\n598\t        eprintln!(\"\\n✅ Configuration complete!\");\n599\t        eprintln!(\"   Config: {}\", config_path.display());\n600\t        eprintln!(\"   Launcher: {}\", launcher_path.display());\n601\t    }\n602\t\n603\t    info!(\n604\t        \"Onboarding complete: using {} backend\",\n605\t        selected_tool.name\n606\t    );\n607\t    Ok(())\n608\t}\n609\t\n610\t/// Normalize backend name for comparison.\n611\t///\n612\t/// Maps common aliases to canonical names:\n613\t/// - \"claude\", \"claude-code\", \"claudecode\" -> \"claude-code\"\n614\t/// - \"opencode\" -> \"opencode\"\n615\t/// - \"aider\" -> \"aider\"\n616\tfn normalize_backend_name(name: &str) -> String {\n617\t    let lower = name.to_lowercase();\n618\t    match lower.as_str() {\n619\t        \"claude\" | \"claude-code\" | \"claudecode\" => \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n620\t        other => other.to_string(),\n621\t    }\n622\t}\n623\t\n624\t/// Handle the `forge init` command.\n625\tfn handle_init_command(non_interactive: bool, force: bool, reconfigure: bool, detect_tools: bool) -> ExitCode {\n626\t    info!(\n627\t        \"Handling init command (non_interactive={}, force={}, reconfigure={}, detect_tools={})\",\n628\t        non_interactive, force, reconfigure, detect_tools\n629\t    );\n630\t\n631\t    // Handle --detect-tools: dry run that only shows detection results\n632\t    if detect_tools {\n633\t        return handle_detect_tools_command();\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    let forge_dir = get_forge_dir();\n637\t    let config_path = forge_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n638\t\n639\t    // Handle --reconfigure: backup existing config before re-running setup\n640\t    if reconfigure {\n641\t        if !config_path.exists() {\n642\t            if non_interactive {\n643\t                eprintln!(\"error: No configuration found to reconfigure\");\n644\t                eprintln!(\"Use 'forge init' to create initial configuration\");\n645\t            } else {\n646\t                eprintln!(\"No configuration found. Running first-time setup instead...\\n\");\n647\t            }\n648\t            // Fall through to normal setup since there's nothing to reconfigure\n649\t        } else {\n650\t            // Backup existing config before proceeding\n651\t            if let Err(e) = backup_config(&forge_dir, &config_path) {\n652\t                eprintln!(\"❌ Failed to backup config: {}\", e);\n653\t                return ExitCode::from(1);\n654\t            }\n655\t            if !non_interactive {\n656\t                eprintln!(\"📦 Existing configuration backed up\\n\");\n657\t            }\n658\t        }\n659\t    } else if !force && config_path.exists() {\n660\t        // Neither --force nor --reconfigure: don't overwrite existing config\n661\t        if non_interactive {\n662\t            eprintln!(\n663\t                \"error: Configuration already exists at {}\",\n664\t                config_path.display()\n665\t            );\n666\t            eprintln!(\"Use --force to overwrite existing configuration\");\n667\t            eprintln!(\"Use --reconfigure to backup and re-run setup\");\n668\t            return ExitCode::from(1);\n669\t        } else {\n670\t            eprintln!(\n671\t                \"Configuration already exists at {}\",\n672\t                config_path.display()\n673\t            );\n674\t            eprintln!(\"Use --force to overwrite existing configuration\");\n675\t            eprintln!(\"Use --reconfigure to backup and re-run setup\");\n676\t            return ExitCode::SUCCESS;\n677\t        }\n678\t    }\n679\t\n680\t    // Get preferred backend from environment variable\n681\t    let preferred_backend = std::env::var(\"FORGE_CHAT_BACKEND\").ok();\n682\t\n683\t    if non_interactive {\n684\t        // Non-interactive mode: quiet output, clear error messages to stderr\n685\t        match run_onboarding(true, preferred_backend.as_deref()) {\n686\t            Ok(()) => ExitCode::SUCCESS,\n687\t            Err(_) => ExitCode::from(1),\n688\t        }\n689\t    } else {\n690\t        // Interactive mode: show welcome message and full output\n691\t        eprintln!(\"🚀 FORGE Setup\");\n692\t        eprintln!();\n693\t\n694\t        match run_onboarding(false, preferred_backend.as_deref()) {\n695\t            Ok(()) => {\n696\t                eprintln!(\"\\n✅ Setup complete!\");\n697\t                ExitCode::SUCCESS\n698\t            }\n699\t            Err(e) => {\n700\t                eprintln!(\"❌ Setup failed: {}\", e);\n701\t                ExitCode::from(1)\n702\t            }\n703\t        }\n704\t    }\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\t/// Backup existing config.yaml before reconfiguration.\n708\t///\n709\t/// Creates a timestamped backup to preserve user's previous settings.\n710\t/// Keeps logs, costs database, and status files intact.\n711\tfn backup_config(forge_dir: &std::path::Path, config_path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n712\t    let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\");\n713\t    let backup_path = forge_dir.join(format!(\"config.yaml.backup.{}\", timestamp));\n714\t\n715\t    std::fs::copy(config_path, &backup_path)?;\n716\t    info!(\"Backed up config to: {}\", backup_path.display());\n717\t\n718\t    // Check for launcher scripts that might have custom modifications\n719\t    let launchers_dir = forge_dir.join(\"launchers\");\n720\t    if launchers_dir.exists()\n721\t        && let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&launchers_dir)\n722\t    {\n723\t        for entry in entries.flatten() {\n724\t            let path = entry.path();\n725\t            if path.is_file() {\n726\t                // Check if the file contains any comments that suggest custom modifications\n727\t                if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&path)\n728\t                    && (content.contains(\"# CUSTOM:\") || content.contains(\"# USER:\") || content.contains(\"# Modified by\")) {\n729\t                        eprintln!(\"⚠️  Warning: {} may contain custom modifications\", path.display());\n730\t                        // Backup the launcher script too\n731\t                        let launcher_backup = forge_dir.join(format!(\n732\t                            \"launchers/{}.backup.{}\",\n733\t                            entry.file_name().to_string_lossy(),\n734\t                            timestamp\n735\t                        ));\n736\t                        let _ = std::fs::copy(&path, &launcher_backup);\n737\t                    }\n738\t            }\n739\t        }\n740\t    }\n741\t\n742\t    Ok(backup_path)\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Handle the `forge init --detect-tools` command.\n746\t///\n747\t/// Runs CLI tool detection and displays results without modifying any files.\n748\tfn handle_detect_tools_command() -> ExitCode {\n749\t    info!(\"Running CLI tool detection (dry run)\");\n750\t\n751\t    eprintln!(\"🔍 Detecting available CLI tools...\\n\");\n752\t\n753\t    match detection::detect_cli_tools_with_diagnostics() {\n754\t        Ok((tools, diagnostics)) => {\n755\t            // Display all detected tools\n756\t            if tools.is_empty() {\n757\t                eprintln!(\"No CLI tools found.\\n\");\n758\t            } else {\n759\t                eprintln!(\"Detected CLI tools:\\n\");\n760\t                for tool in &tools {\n761\t                    let status_icon = match tool.status {\n762\t                        detection::ToolStatus::Ready => \"✅\",\n763\t                        detection::ToolStatus::MissingApiKey => \"⚠️ \",\n764\t                        detection::ToolStatus::IncompatibleVersion => \"❌\",\n765\t                        detection::ToolStatus::NotExecutable => \"❌\",\n766\t                    };\n767\t\n768\t                    eprintln!(\"  {} {} ({})\", status_icon, tool.name, tool.status_message());\n769\t                    eprintln!(\"      Path: {}\", tool.binary_path.display());\n770\t                    if let Some(version) = &tool.version {\n771\t                        eprintln!(\"      Version: {}\", version);\n772\t                    }\n773\t                    eprintln!(\"      Headless: {} | Skip-Perms: {}\",\n774\t                        if tool.headless_support { \"yes\" } else { \"no\" },\n775\t                        if tool.skip_permissions { \"yes\" } else { \"no\" }\n776\t                    );\n777\t                    if tool.api_key_required {\n778\t                        let key_status = if tool.api_key_detected { \"✅ found\" } else { \"❌ missing\" };\n779\t                        if let Some(env_var) = &tool.api_key_env_var {\n780\t                            eprintln!(\"      API Key: {} ({})\", key_status, env_var);\n781\t                        } else {\n782\t                            eprintln!(\"      API Key: {}\", key_status);\n783\t                        }\n784\t                    }\n785\t                    eprintln!();\n786\t                }\n787\t            }\n788\t\n789\t            // Display diagnostics about rejected/not-found tools\n790\t            if !diagnostics.rejections.is_empty() {\n791\t                eprintln!(\"Detection details:\");\n792\t                for rejection in &diagnostics.rejections {\n793\t                    match &rejection.reason {\n794\t                        guidance::RejectionReason::NotFound => {\n795\t                            eprintln!(\"  • {} - not found in PATH\", rejection.tool_name);\n796\t                        }\n797\t                        guidance::RejectionReason::MissingApiKey { env_var, .. } => {\n798\t                            eprintln!(\"  • {} - missing API key ({})\", rejection.tool_name, env_var);\n799\t                        }\n800\t                        guidance::RejectionReason::IncompatibleVersion { version, missing_feature, .. } => {\n801\t                            let ver = version.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n802\t                            eprintln!(\"  • {} {} - incompatible: {}\", rejection.tool_name, ver, missing_feature);\n803\t                        }\n804\t                        guidance::RejectionReason::NotExecutable(_) => {\n805\t                            eprintln!(\"  • {} - not executable\", rejection.tool_name);\n806\t                        }\n807\t                    }\n808\t                }\n809\t                eprintln!();\n810\t            }\n811\t\n812\t            // Summary\n813\t            let ready_count = tools.iter().filter(|t| t.is_ready()).count();\n814\t            if ready_count > 0 {\n815\t                eprintln!(\"✅ {} tool(s) ready for use\", ready_count);\n816\t                eprintln!(\"   Run 'forge init' to configure\");\n817\t            } else {\n818\t                eprintln!(\"❌ No compatible tools ready\");\n819\t                eprintln!(\"   Install Claude Code, OpenCode, or configure API keys\");\n820\t            }\n821\t\n822\t            ExitCode::SUCCESS\n823\t        }\n824\t        Err(e) => {\n825\t            eprintln!(\"❌ Detection failed: {}\", e);\n826\t            ExitCode::from(1)\n827\t        }\n828\t    }\n829\t}\n830\t\n831\t/// Validate the config file and offer recovery if invalid.\n832\tfn validate_config() -> Result<(), String> {\n833\t    use std::io::{self, Write};\n834\t\n835\t    match ForgeConfig::load_with_error() {\n836\t        Ok(_config) => {\n837\t            info!(\"Configuration validated successfully\");\n838\t            Ok(())\n839\t        }\n840\t        Err(e) => {\n841\t            // Format the error with line/column information\n842\t            eprintln!(\"❌ Configuration Error\");\n843\t            eprintln!();\n844\t\n845\t            let path_str = e.path().map(|p| p.display().to_string())\n846\t                .unwrap_or_else(|| \"~/.forge/config.yaml\".to_string());\n847\t\n848\t            if let (Some(line), Some(col)) = (e.line_number(), e.column_number()) {\n849\t                eprintln!(\"  File: {}\", path_str);\n850\t                eprintln!(\"  Line: {}, Column: {}\", line, col);\n851\t                eprintln!(\"  Error: {}\", e);\n852\t            } else {\n853\t                eprintln!(\"  {}\", e);\n854\t            }\n855\t            eprintln!();\n856\t\n857\t            // Offer recovery options\n858\t            eprintln!(\"Recovery options:\");\n859\t            eprintln!(\"  1) Reset to default configuration (creates backup)\");\n860\t            eprintln!(\"  2) Use defaults without modifying file (temporary)\");\n861\t            eprintln!(\"  3) Exit and fix manually\");\n862\t            eprintln!();\n863\t            eprint!(\"Choose option [1-3]: \");\n864\t            io::stdout().flush().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n865\t\n866\t            let mut choice = String::new();\n867\t            io::stdin().read_line(&mut choice).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n868\t\n869\t            match choice.trim() {\n870\t                \"1\" => {\n871\t                    if let Err(e) = reset_config_to_defaults() {\n872\t                        return Err(format!(\"Failed to reset config: {}\", e));\n873\t                    }\n874\t                    eprintln!(\"✅ Configuration reset to defaults\");\n875\t                    eprintln!(\"   Previous config backed up\");\n876\t                    eprintln!();\n877\t                    Ok(())\n878\t                }\n879\t                \"2\" => {\n880\t                    eprintln!(\"⚠️  Using default configuration (file not modified)\");\n881\t                    eprintln!(\"   Fix the config file manually or delete it to regenerate\");\n882\t                    eprintln!();\n883\t                    Ok(())\n884\t                }\n885\t                \"3\" | \"\" => {\n886\t                    Err(\"Exiting. Please fix the config file and try again.\".to_string())\n887\t                }\n888\t                _ => {\n889\t                    Err(\"Invalid choice. Exiting.\".to_string())\n890\t                }\n891\t            }\n892\t        }\n893\t    }\n894\t}\n895\t\n896\t/// Reset config file to defaults with backup.\n897\tfn reset_config_to_defaults() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n898\t    let forge_dir = get_forge_dir();\n899\t    let config_path = forge_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n900\t\n901\t    // Create backup\n902\t    if config_path.exists() {\n903\t        let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\");\n904\t        let backup_path = forge_dir.join(format!(\"config.yaml.backup.{}\", timestamp));\n905\t        std::fs::copy(&config_path, &backup_path)?;\n906\t        info!(\"Created config backup: {}\", backup_path.display());\n907\t    }\n908\t\n909\t    // Generate default config\n910\t    let default_config = r#\"# FORGE Configuration\n911\t# This file was automatically generated after a config error.\n912\t\n913\tdashboard:\n914\t  refresh_interval_ms: 1000\n915\t  max_fps: 60\n916\t  default_layout: overview\n917\t\n918\ttheme:\n919\t  name: default\n920\t\n921\tcost_tracking:\n922\t  enabled: true\n923\t  budget_warning_threshold: 70\n924\t  budget_critical_threshold: 90\n925\t  # monthly_budget_usd: 100.0\n926\t\n927\t# Chat backend configuration (optional)\n928\t# Uncomment and configure if using the chat feature:\n929\t# chat_backend:\n930\t#   command: claude-code\n931\t#   args:\n932\t#     - --headless\n933\t#     - --model\n934\t#     - sonnet\n935\t#   model: sonnet\n936\t\"#;\n937\t\n938\t    std::fs::write(&config_path, default_config)?;\n939\t    info!(\"Reset config to defaults: {}\", config_path.display());\n940\t    Ok(())\n941\t}\n942\t\n943\t/// Handle worker subcommand actions.\n944\tfn handle_worker_action(action: &WorkerAction) -> ExitCode {\n945\t    let writer = match StatusWriter::new(None) {\n946\t        Ok(w) => w,\n947\t        Err(e) => {\n948\t            eprintln!(\"❌ Failed to initialize status writer: {}\", e);\n949\t            return ExitCode::from(1);\n950\t        }\n951\t    };\n952\t\n953\t    match action {\n954\t        WorkerAction::Pause { id } => {\n955\t            if id.to_lowercase() == \"all\" {\n956\t                match writer.pause_all() {\n957\t                    Ok(count) => {\n958\t                        if count == 0 {\n959\t                            eprintln!(\"No workers to pause\");\n960\t                        } else {\n961\t                            eprintln!(\"⏸️  Paused {} worker(s)\", count);\n962\t                        }\n963\t                        ExitCode::SUCCESS\n964\t                    }\n965\t                    Err(e) => {\n966\t                        eprintln!(\"❌ Failed to pause workers: {}\", e);\n967\t                        ExitCode::from(1)\n968\t                    }\n969\t                }\n970\t            } else {\n971\t                match writer.pause_worker(id) {\n972\t                    Ok(()) => {\n973\t                        eprintln!(\"⏸️  Paused worker: {}\", id);\n974\t                        ExitCode::SUCCESS\n975\t                    }\n976\t                    Err(e) => {\n977\t                        eprintln!(\"❌ Failed to pause worker '{}': {}\", id, e);\n978\t                        ExitCode::from(1)\n979\t                    }\n980\t                }\n981\t            }\n982\t        }\n983\t        WorkerAction::Resume { id } => {\n984\t            if id.to_lowercase() == \"all\" {\n985\t                match writer.resume_all()  let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n295\t        *running = false;\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    /// Start the server.\n299\t    pub async fn run(self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n300\t        // Set running flag\n301\t        {\n302\t            let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n303\t            *running = true;\n304\t        }\n305\t\n306\t        let app = Router::new()\n307\t            .route(\"/ws\", get(ws_handler))\n308\t            .route(\"/health\", get(health_handler))\n309\t            .with_state(self.clone());\n310\t\n311\t        let addr = format!(\"{}:{}\", self.config.bind_address, self.config.port);\n312\t        info!(\"FORGE server listening on {}\", addr);\n313\t\n314\t        let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(&addr)\n315\t            .await\n316\t            .map_err(|e| ServerError::Io(e))?;\n317\t\n318\t        axum::serve(listener, app)\n319\t            .await\n320\t            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(e.to_string()))?;\n321\t\n322\t        // Clear running flag on shutdown\n323\t        {\n324\t            let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n325\t            *running = false;\n326\t        }\n327\t\n328\t        Ok(())\n329\t    }\n330\t\n331\t    /// Handle a WebSocket connection.\n332\t    async fn handle_connection(\n333\t        self,\n334\t        socket: WebSocket,\n335\t        addr: SocketAddr,\n336\t    ) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n337\t        let (mut sender, mut receiver) = socket.split();\n338\t        let mut session_id: Option<String> = None;\n339\t        let _rx = self.subscribe();\n340\t\n341\t        // Send initial welcome after auth\n342\t        let mut authenticated = false;\n343\t\n344\t        while let Some(result) = receiver.next().await {\n345\t            match result {\n346\t                Ok(msg) => {\n347\t                    match msg {\n348\t                        Message::Text(text) => {\n349\t                            if let Ok(client_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ClientMessage>(&text) {\n350\t                                match client_msg {\n351\t                                    ClientMessage::Authenticate { user_id, credentials } => {\n352\t                                        match self.auth.authenticate(&user_id, &credentials).await {\n353\t                                            Ok(auth_result) => {\n354\t                                                let session = self.session_registry.manager()\n355\t                                                    .create_session(\n356\t                                                        &user_id,\n357\t                                                        &auth_result.display_name,\n358\t                                                        auth_result.role,\n359\t                                                    )\n360\t                                                    .await?;\n361\t\n362\t                                                session_id = Some(session.session_id.clone());\n363\t\n364\t                                                let welcome = ServerMessage::Welcome {\n365\t                                                    session: session.clone(),\n366\t                                                    server_info: ServerInfo {\n367\t                                                        server_version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n368\t                                                        connected_users: self.session_registry.manager().session_count().await,\n369\t                                                        active_workers: {\n370\t                                                            let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n371\t                                                            state.workers.len()\n372\t                                                        },\n373\t                                                        pending_beads: {\n374\t                                                            let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n375\t                                                            state.beads.iter().filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::Open).count()\n376\t                                                        },\n377\t                                                    },\n378\t                                                };\n379\t\n380\t                                                let _ = sender.send(Message::Text(\n381\t                                                    serde_json::to_string(&welcome).unwrap().into()\n382\t                                                )).await;\n383\t\n384\t                                                // Broadcast user joined\n385\t                                                self.broadcast(ServerMessage::UserJoined {\n386\t                                                    user: user_id.clone(),\n387\t                                                    display_name: auth_result.display_name,\n388\t                                                    role: auth_result.role,\n389\t                                                });\n390\t\n391\t                                                authenticated = true;\n392\t                                                info!(\"User {} authenticated from {}\", user_id, addr);\n393\t                                            }\n394\t                                            Err(e) => {\n395\t                                                let error_msg = ServerMessage::Error {\n396\t                                                    message: format!(\"Authentication failed: {}\", e),\n397\t                                                };\n398\t                                                let _ = sender.send(Message::Text(\n399\t                                                    serde_json::to_string(&error_msg).unwrap().into()\n400\t                                                )).await;\n401\t                                            }\n402\t                                        }\n403\t                                    }\n404\t                                    ClientMessage::Pong => {\n405\t                                        // Keep connection alive\n406\t                                        if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {\n407\t                                            let _ = self.session_registry.manager().update_activity(sid).await;\n408\t                                        }\n409\t                                        if authenticated {\n410\t                                            // Respond with ping to keep connection alive\n411\t                                            let _ = sender.send(Message::Text(\n412\t                                                serde_json::to_string(&ServerMessage::Ping).unwrap().into()\n413\t                                            )).await;\n414\t                                        }\n415\t                                    }\n416\t                                    ClientMessage::UpdateView { view } => {\n417\t                                        if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {\n418\t                                            let _ = self.session_registry.manager().update_view(sid, &view).await;\n419\t                                        }\n420\t                                    }\n421\t                                    ClientMessage::AssignBead { bead_id, to } => {\n422\t                                        if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {\n423\t                                            if let Some(session) = self.session_registry.manager().get_session(sid).await {\n424\t                                                if session.role.can_assign_beads() {\n425\t                                                    if let Ok(assignment) = self.assignment_tracker.assign(\n426\t                                                        &bead_id, &to, &session.user_id\n427\t                                                    ).await {\n428\t                                                        // Log the bead assignment to audit log\n429\t                                                        if let Some(ref logger) = self.audit_logger {\n430\t                                                            let _ = logger.log(AuditEvent::new(\n431\t                                                                EventType::UserAction,\n432\t                                                                &session.user_id,\n433\t                                                                \"bead_assignment\",\n434\t                                                                &bead_id,\n435\t                                                            )\n436\t                                                            .with_new_value(format!(\"assigned_to={}\", &to))\n437\t                                                            .with_metadata(format!(\"assigned_by={}\", &session.user_id)));\n438\t                                                        }\n439\t                                                        self.broadcast(ServerMessage::BeadAssigned {\n440\t                                                            bead_id,\n441\t                                                            assigned_to:                             });\n444\t                                                    }\n445\t                                                }\n446\t                                            }\n447\t                                        }\n448\t                                    }\n449\t                                    ClientMessage::UnassignBead { bead_id } => {\n450\t                                              if session.role.can_assign_beads() {\n453\t                                                    if let Ok(Some(assignment)) =                                                  .with_old_value(format!(\"was_assigned_to={}\", &assignment.assigned_to.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"<none>\"))));\n463\t                                                        }\n464\t                                                    }\n465\t                                                }\n466\t                                            }\n467\t                                        }\n468\t                                    }\n469\t                                    _ => {\n470\t                                        debug!(\"Unhandled client message: {:?}\", client_msg);\n471\t                                    }\n472\t                                }\n473\t                            }\n474\t                        }\n475\t                        Message::Close(_) => {\n476\t                            debug!(\"Client {} closing connection\", addr);\n477\t                            break;\n478\t                        }\n479\t                        _ => {}\n480\t                    }\n481\t                }\n482\t                Err(e) => {\n483\t                    warn!(\"WebSocket error from {}: {}\", addr, e);\n484\t                    break;\n485\t                }\n486\t            }\n487\t        }\n488\t\n489\t        // Cleanup on disconnect\n490\t        if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n491\t            if let {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b55fb2a6547147ecaf7db431","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n4\t//! and coordinates between different components.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n9\t//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n10\t//!\n11\t//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n12\t//!\n13\t//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n14\t//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n15\t//!    chat operations.\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n18\t//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n19\t//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n20\t//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n21\t//!\n22\t//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n23\t//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n24\t//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n25\t//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n26\t//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n27\t//!\n28\t//! ## State Management\n29\t//!\n30\t//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n31\t//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n32\t//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n33\t//!\n34\t//! ## Error Handling\n35\t//!\n36\t//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n37\t//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n38\t//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n39\t//!\n40\t//! ## Performance\n41\t//!\n42\t//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n43\t//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n44\t//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n45\t\n46\tuse std::io;\n47\tuse std::path::PathBuf;\n48\tuse {\n413\t    /// Duration in milliseconds\n414\t    #[serde(default)]\n415\t    pub duration_ms: u64,\n416\t    /// Estimated cost in USD\n417\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n418\t    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n419\t    /// Provider name\n420\t    #[serde(default)]\n421\t    pub provider: String,\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\n425\tfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n426\t    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n427\t\n428\t    // Config not found errors\n429\t    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n430\t        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    // API/authentication errors\n434\t    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || error_lower.contains(\"unauthorized\") {\n435\t        return Some(\"Check your API key in ~/.forge/config.yaml. Ensure the key is valid and not expired.\".to_string());\n436\t    }\n437\t\n438\t    // Rate limit errors\n439\t    if error_lower.contains(\"rate limit\") || error_lower.contains(\"too many requests\") {\n440\t        return Some(\"Wait a moment before sending more commands. Rate limits reset automatically.\".to_string());\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    // Network unreachable (no internet)\n444\t    if error_lower.contains(\"network unreachable\") || error_lower.contains(\"no route to host\") {\n445\t        return Some(\"⚠️  Network unreachable. Check your internet connection and try again when online. Last known state is cached.\".to_string());\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    // DNS resolution failures\n449\t    if error_lower.contains(\"dns\") || error_lower.contains(\"failed to lookup address\") || error_lower.contains(\"name or service not known\") {\n450\t        return Some(\"⚠️  DNS resolution failed. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_12e1d62e0ced404287cfc8fc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# FORGE Development Guide for Claude Code\n2\t\n3\tThis document provides context and procedures for AI assistants working on the FORGE project.\n4\t\n5\t## Project Overview\n6\t\n7\tFORGE is a terminal-based Agent Orchestration Dashboard written in Rust using ratatui. It manages AI worker agents, tracks tasks/beads, monitors costs, and includes a conversational chat interface.\n8\t\n9\t- **Language**: Rust (edition 2024, requires 1.88+)\n10\t- **TUI Framework**: ratatui 0.29 with crossterm 0.28\n11\t- **Async Runtime**: tokio 1.43\n12\t- **Database**: SQLite via rusqlite (bundled)\n13\t- **Version**: 0.1.9 (workspace.version)\n14\t\n15\t## Development Workflow\n16\t\n17\t### Building\n18\t\n19\t```bash\n20\t# Debug build (faster compilation)\n21\tcargo build\n22\t\n23\t# Release build (optimized, what gets shipped)\n24\tcargo build --release\n25\t\n26\t# Run tests\n27\tcargo test\n28\t\n29\t# Run with logging\n30\tcargo run -- --debug\n31\t\n32\t# Run release binary directly\n33\t./target/release/forge\n34\t```\n35\t\n36\t### Testing in Tmux Sessions\n37\t\n38\t**CRITICAL**: Always test the TUI in a separate tmux session, never inside Claude Code itself.\n39\t\n40\t**Why**: The TUI uses alternate screen mode and raw terminal mode. Testing within Claude Code's terminal emulator can cause:\n41\t- Visual artifacts (ghost text, misalignment)\n42\t- Key capture issues\n43\t- Screen corruption on exit\n44\t- Terminal state not restored properly\n45\t\n46\t**IMPORTANT: Test in Multiple Terminal Dimensions**\n47\t\n48\tVisual artifacts often appear only at specific terminal sizes. FORGE has layout modes:\n49\t- **Narrow**: < 120 columns (single-view mode)\n50\t- **Wide**: 120-198 columns (2-column mode)\n51\t- **UltraWide**: 199+ columns (3-column mode with all panels)\n52\t\n53\tAlways test at **multiple dimensions** to catch layout-specific bugs:\n54\t\n55\t```bash\n56\t# Test at narrow size (common for small terminals / split panes)\n57\ttmux new-session -d -s forge-narrow -x 80 -y 24\n58\t\n59\t# Test at standard wide size\n60\ttmux new-session -d -s forge-wide -x 140 -y 40\n61\t\n62\t# Test at ultra-wide size\n63\ttmux new-session -d -s forge-ultrawide -x 200 -y 50\n64\t\n65\t# Test at minimum viable size\n66\ttmux new-session -d -s forge-min -x 60 -y 20\n67\t```\n68\t\n69\t**Common dimension-specific issues**:\n70\t- Text overflow in narrow mode (< 80 cols)\n71\t- Panel clipping when height < 30 rows\n72\t- Timestamp misalignment at narrow widths\n73\t- Chat history truncation in small areas\n74\t\n75\t**How to test correctly**:\n76\t\n77\t```bash\n78\t# 1. Build the project\n79\tcd /home/coder/forge\n80\tcargo build --release\n81\t\n82\t# 2. Create a fresh tmux session for testing\n83\ttmux new-session -d -s forge-test -x 120 -y 40\n84\t\n85\t# 3. Run forge in the test session\n86\ttmux send-keys -t forge-test \"cd /home/coder/forge && ./target/release/forge --debug\" Enter\n87\t\n88\t# 4. Attach to interact\n89\ttmux attach -t forge-test\n90\t\n91\t# 5. When done, detach (Ctrl+B, D) or kill (Ctrl+B, :kill-session)\n92\t```\n93\t\n94\t**Automated testing script**: Use `test-forge-chat.sh` for chat UI validation:\n95\t```bash\n96\t./test-forge-chat.sh\n97\t# This script:\n98\t# - Creates a tmux session\n99\t# - Starts forge\n100\t# - Switches to Chat view\n101\t# - Submits test queries\n102\t# - Validates rendering and responses\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t**IMPORTANT: Cleanup Test Sessions**\n106\t\n107\tAlways clean up tmux sessions after testing to prevent accumulation:\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Kill your test session when done\n111\ttmux kill-session -t forge-test\n112\t\n113\t# Clean up all forge-related test sessions\n114\ttmux list-sessions | grep '^forge-' | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -I{} tmux kill-session -t {}\n115\t\n116\t# The test framework provides cleanup helpers (see tests/lib/test-helpers.sh)\n117\tstop_forge \"$session\"           # Clean up main test session\n118\tcleanup_spawned_workers         # Clean up worker sessions spawned during test\n119\t```\n120\t\n121\tFor detailed testing architecture and cleanup requirements, see:\n122\t- **[ADR 0017: Tmux-Based Testing with Agent Control and Cleanup](docs/adr/0017-tmux-based-testing-with-cleanup.md)**\n123\t- **[Bead bd-3er6](https://github.com/jedarden/forge/issues)** - Implements cleanup for test-forge-workers.sh\n124\t\n125\t### Tmux Testing Commands\n126\t\n127\t```bash\n128\t# List active sessions\n129\ttmux list-sessions\n130\t\n131\t# Attach to a session\n132\ttmux attach -t <session-name>\n133\t\n134\t# Kill a session\n135\ttmux kill-session -t <session-name>\n136\t\n137\t# Capture pane content (for debugging)\n138\ttmux capture-pane -t <session-name> -p\n139\t\n140\t# Send keys to a session (for automated testing)\n141\ttmux send-keys -t <session-name> \"command\" Enter\n142\t```\n143\t\n144\t### Creating GitHub Releases\n145\t\n146\t**Prerequisites**:\n147\t- All tests passing\n148\t- Version updated in `Cargo.toml` (workspace.version)\n149\t- CHANGELOG.md updated with release notes\n150\t- Binary tested in real tmux session\n151\t\n152\t**Release Process**:\n153\t\n154\t```bash\n155\t# 1. Update version in workspace Cargo.toml\n156\t# Edit workspace.package.version = \"0.x.y\"\n157\t\n158\t# 2. Update CHANGELOG.md\n159\t# Add new section with release notes\n160\t\n161\t# 3. Add variant to `View` enum in `crates/forge-tui/src/view.rs`\n230\t2. Add hotkey mapping in `View::hotkey()`\n231\t3. Add title in `View::title()`\n232\t4. Implement `draw_<view>()` method in `app.rs`\n233\t5. Add case to main `draw()` method match statement\n234\t6. Update `View::ALL` array\n235\t7. Test navigation and rendering\n236\t\n237\t### Adding Chat Commands\n238\t\n239\t1. Parse command in `app.rs` event handler (`handle_key()`)\n240\t2. Extract command arguments from `self.chat_input`\n241\t3. Execute command logic\n242\t4. Format response for chat history\n243\t5. Test command execution in Chat view\n244\t\n245\t## Git Workflow\n246\t\n247\t```bash\n248\t# Create feature branch\n249\tgit checkout -b feature/my-feature\n250\t\n251\t# Make changes and commit\n252\tgit add .\n253\tgit commit -m \"feat: add my feature\"\n254\t\n255\t# Push to GitHub\n256\tgit push origin feature/my-feature\n257\t\n258\t# Create PR via GitHub web UI\n259\t# https://github.com/jedarden/forge/compare\n260\t```\n261\t\n262\t## Useful Commands\n263\t\n264\t```bash\n265\t# Format code\n266\tcargo fmt\n267\t\n268\t# Lint code\n269\tcargo clippy -- -D warnings\n270\t\n271\t# Run all tests\n272\tcargo test --workspace\n273\t\n274\t# Run specific test\n275\tcargo test --package forge-tui test_name\n276\t\n277\t# Build documentation\n278\tcargo doc --open\n279\t\n280\t# Check for unused dependencies\n281\tcargo +nightly udeps\n282\t```\n283\t\n284\t## Contact\n285\t\n286\t- **Repository**: https://github.com/jedarden/forge\n287\t- **Issues**: https://github.com/jedarden/forge/issues\n288\t- **Discussions**: https://github.com/jedarden/forge/discussions\n289\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_e39fa264abe34ae29540c5c8","session_id":"48b5c44d-e779-450f-9849-828e3185fc4c","uuid":"230f8533-414e-44c8-b18a-2e798f1a2848","timestamp":"2026-05-22T20:14:16.903Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore forge-server integration"}

Looking at `run_client_background_task`:\n\n1. It creates a ForgeClient\n2. It subscribes to state updates: `let mut state_rx = client.subscribe();`\n3. It connects: `client.connect_and_run().await`\n4. But **there's no loop that actually receives messages from `state_rx` and forwards them to `response_tx`!**\n\nThe background task just connects and then exits. It doesn't actually:\n- Read messages from `state_rx` \n- Forward them to `response_tx` so the TUI can receive them\n- Handle incoming requests from `async_req_rx`\n\nThis is the core issue. The `run_client_background_task` needs to:\n1. Spawn a task to forward state updates from `state_rx` to `response_tx`\n2. info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n80\t\n81\t        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n82\t            .await\n83\t            .map_err(|e| ws_stream.split();\n88\t\n89\t        // Create channel for sending messages\n90\t        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n91\t        *self.write_tx.lock().await =     }\n108\t        });\n109\t\n110\t        // Send authentication\n111\t        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n112\t            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n113\t     }\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n239\t    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n240\t        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n241\t WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(mut self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: view.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Request full state sync.\n    pub async fn request_sync(&self) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Snapshot of the current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientStateSnapshot {\n    pub session: Option<UserSession>,\n    pub server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    pub state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    pub connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    pub authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_client_config() {\n        let config = ClientConfig {\n            server_url: \"ws://localhost:8080/ws\".to_string(),\n            user_id: \"testuser\".to_string(),\n            password: \"testpass\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(config.server_url, \"ws://localhost:8080/ws\");\n        assert_eq!(config.user_id, \"testuser\");\n        assert_eq!(config.password, \"testpass\");\n    }\n}\n","numLines":340,"startLine":1,"totalLines":340}}}

The `connect_and_run()` method runs the WebSocket connection loop\n\nThe issue in `run_client_background_task` is:\n1. It gets `state_rx` from `client.subscribe()` but **never reads from it**\n2.
