WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(mut self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: view.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Request full state sync.\n    pub async fn request_sync(&self) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Snapshot of the current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientStateSnapshot {\n    pub session: Option<UserSession>,\n    pub server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    pub state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    pub connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    pub authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_client_config() {\n        let config = ClientConfig {\n            server_url: WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(mut self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the write channel for sending messages to the server.\n    ///\n    /// This returns the broadcast sender that can be used to send messages\n    /// from outside the client connection loop. The sender becomes available\n    /// after the connection is established.\n    pub fn get_write_channel(&self) -> tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>> {\n        // Create a watch channel to notify when write_tx is ready\n        let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::watch::channel(None);\n        let write_tx = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            // Wait for write_tx to be set\n            loop {\n                let guard = write_tx.lock().await;\n                if guard.is_some() {\n                    let _ = tx.send(guard.clone());\n                    break;\n                }\n                drop(guard);\n                tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n            }\n        });\n        rx\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: It should be spawned as a background task.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(&self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {","originalFile":"//! WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(mut self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message directly from outside the client connection loop.\n    ///\n    /// This method can be called from external tasks to send messages to the server.\n    /// It will return an error if the connection is not yet established.\n    pub async fn send_direct(&self, msg: ClientMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let write_tx = self.write_tx.lock().await;\n        if let Some(tx) = write_tx.as_ref() {\n            let _ = tx.send(msg);\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Connection not established\".to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    ///\n    /// This method runs the WebSocket connection loop and will block until\n    /// the connection is closed. It should be spawned as a background task.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(&self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message directly from outside the client connection loop.\n    ///\n    /// This method can be called from external tasks to send messages to the server.\n    /// It will return an error if the connection is not yet established.\n    pub async fn send_direct(&self, msg: ClientMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let write_tx = self.write_tx.lock().await;\n        if let Some(tx) = write_tx.as_ref() {\n            let _ = tx.send(msg);\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Connection not established\".to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: view.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Request full state sync.\n    pub async fn request_sync(&self) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Snapshot of the current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientStateSnapshot {\n    pub session: Option<UserSession>,\n    pub server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    pub state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    pub connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    pub authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    ///\n    /// This method runs the WebSocket connection loop and will block until\n    /// the connection is closed. It should be spawned as a background task.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(&self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message directly from outside the client connection loop.\n    ///\n    /// This method can be called from external tasks to send messages to the server.\n    /// It will return an error if the connection is not yet established.\n    pub async fn send_direct(&self, msg: ClientMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let write_tx = self.write_tx.lock().await;\n        if let Some(tx) = write_tx.as_ref() {\n            let _ = tx.send(msg);\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Connection not established\".to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: view.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Request full state sync.\n    pub async fn request_sync(&self) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Snapshot of the current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientStateSnapshot {\n    pub session: Option<UserSession>,\n    pub server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    pub state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    pub connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    pub authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n//! and coordinates between different components.\n//!\n//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n//!\n//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n//!\n//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n//!\n//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n//!    chat operations.\n//!\n//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n//!\n//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n//!\n//! ## State Management\n//!\n//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n//!\n//! ## Error Handling\n//!\n//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n//!\n//! ## Performance\n//!\n//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender, TryRecvError};\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse std::thread;\n\nuse serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ChatExchange {\n    pub user_query: String,\n    pub assistant_response: String,\n    pub timestamp: String,\n    pub is_error: bool,\n    /// Tool calls made during this exchange\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallInfo>,\n    /// Side effects from tool execution\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    /// Confirmation prompt if action requires approval\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub confirmation: Option<ConfirmationInfo>,\n    /// Response metadata (duration, cost, provider)\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub metadata: ResponseMetadata,\n    /// Actionable guidance for errors (how to fix)\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error_guidance: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Information about a tool call for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, success: bool,\n    /// Human-readable result message\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Information about a side effect for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct SideEffectInfo {\n    /// Type of effect (e.g., \"spawn\", \"kill\", \"assign\")\n    pub effect_type: String,\n    /// Description of what happened\n    pub description: String,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation prompt information for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ConfirmationInfo {\n    /// Title of the confirmation\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Description of what will happen\n    pub description: String,\n    /// Warning level (info, warning, danger)\n    pub level: ConfirmationLevel,\n    /// Estimated cost impact\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_impact: Option<f64>,\n    /// Items that will be affected\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub affected_items: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether this action is reversible\n    pub reversible: bool,\n}\n\n/// Warning level for confirmation prompts.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub enum ConfirmationLevel {\n    /// Informational (no danger)\n    Info,\n    /// Warning (some risk)\n    Warning,\n    /// Danger (high risk, destructive)\n    Danger,\n}\n\n/// Response metadata for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ResponseMetadata {\n    /// Duration in milliseconds\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n    /// Estimated cost in USD\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n    /// Provider name\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub provider: String,\n}\n\n/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\nfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n\n    // Config not found errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // API/authentication errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || error_lower.contains(\"unauthorized\") {\n        return Some(\"Check your API key in ~/.forge/config.yaml. Ensure the key is valid and not expired.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"rate limit\") || error_lower.contains(\"too many requests\") {\n        return Some(\"Wait a moment before sending more commands. Rate limits reset automatically.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Network unreachable (no internet)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"network unreachable\") || error_lower.contains(\"no route to host\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Network unreachable. Check your internet connection and try again when online. Last known state is cached.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // DNS resolution failures\n    if error_lower.contains(\"dns\") || error_lower.contains(\"failed to lookup address\") || error_lower.contains(\"name or service not known\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  DNS resolution failed. Check DNS settings or try using 8.8.8.8 as your DNS server. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Connection timeout\n    if error_lower.contains(\"timeout\") {\n        return Some(\"⏱️  Request timed out. The server may be slow or unreachable. Click 'r' to retry or check status.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Generic connection/network errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"connection\") || error_lower.contains(\"network\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Connection failed. Check your internet connection. The API server may be temporarily unavailable. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Provider errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"provider\") && error_lower.contains(\"not found\") {\n        return Some(\"Check that the provider is installed and available in your PATH. Run 'forge --help' for setup instructions.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Tool execution errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"tool\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested operation could not be completed. Check the tool output above for details.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Worker errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"worker\") && (error_lower.contains(\"spawn\") || error_lower.contains(\"launch\")) {\n        return Some(\"Ensure you have sufficient system resources and the worker binary is available.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Context gathering errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"context\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The dashboard context could not be loaded. Try restarting the application.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Permission/access errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"permission\") || error_lower.contains(\"access denied\") || error_lower.contains(\"forbidden\") {\n        return Some(\"You don't have permission for this action. Check your configuration and credentials.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Resource not found errors (general)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"does not exist\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested resource was not found. Verify the name and try again.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // JSON parsing errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"json\") || error_lower.contains(\"parse\") || error_lower.contains(\"deserialize\") {\n        return Some(\"There was a problem processing the response. The server may have returned unexpected data.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // IO errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"io error\") || error_lower.contains(\"file\") && error_lower.contains(\"error\") {\n        return Some(\"A file system error occurred. Check file permissions and available disk space.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\n/// Result of an update operation.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum UpdateResult {\n    /// Update completed successfully with version info\n    Success {\n        /// Previous version\n        old_version: String,\n        /// New version\n        new_version: String,\n    },\n    /// Update failed with an error message\n    Failed(String),\n    /// Already up to date\n    AlreadyUpToDate,\n}\n\n/// Progress information for update downloads.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]\npub struct UpdateProgress {\n    /// Current status message\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Download progress percentage (0-100)\n    pub percent: u8,\n    /// Whether the update is in progress\n    pub in_progress: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for App {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl App {\n    /// Create a new app instance with default state.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        use tracing::info;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() started\");\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        // Time DataManager initialization\n        let dm_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Creating DataManager...\");\n        let data_manager = DataManager::new();\n        info!(\"⏱️ DataManager created in {:?}\", dm_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Time ThemeManager initialization\n        let theme_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Loading ThemeManager...\");\n        let theme_manager = ThemeManager::load_config();\n        info!(\"⏱️ ThemeManager loaded in {:?}\", theme_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize worker launcher and runtime (needed before chat backend for worker spawning)\n        let worker_launcher = WorkerLauncher::new();\n        let worker_runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\");\n\n        // Time chat backend initialization\n        let chat_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing chat backend...\");\n        let chat_backend = Self::init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(&worker_runtime, &worker_launcher).map(Arc::new);\n        info!(\"⏱️ Chat backend initialized in {:?}\", chat_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize config watcher for hot-reload (skip in test mode to save inotify instances)\n        let config_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing config watcher...\");\n        let (config_watcher, config_rx, forge_config) = if cfg!(test) {\n            info!(\"⏱️ Skipping config watcher in test mode\");\n            (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n        } else {\n            match ConfigWatcher::new() {\n                Some((watcher, rx)) => {\n                    let config = watcher.current_config().clone();\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher initialized in {:?}\", config_start.elapsed());\n                    (Some(watcher), Some(rx), config)\n                }\n                None => {\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher not initialized (config file not found)\");\n                    (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Calculate initial data poll interval from config\n        let data_poll_interval = Duration::from_millis(\n            forge_config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms.min(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)\n        );\n\n        // Configure alert notifier from config\n        let mut data_manager = data_manager;\n        data_manager.configure_notifier(\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_critical,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_warning,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_interval_secs,\n            forge_config.notifications.visual_flash_on_critical,\n        );\n\n        // Initialize history manager and load previous history\n        let history_manager = forge_chat::HistoryManager::new().ok();\n\n        // Initialize session manager for team collaboration\n        let session_manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager,\n            theme_manager,\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend,\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher,\n            worker_runtime,\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: config_watcher,\n            config_rx,\n            forge_config,\n            data_poll_interval,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager,\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            sessions_panel: crate::sessions_panel::SessionsPanel::new(),\n            server_client_tx: None,\n            server_client_rx: None,\n            user_session: None,\n            session_manager,\n            is_server_connected: false,\n            server_url: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new app with a custom status directory (for testing).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager: DataManager::with_status_dir(status_dir),\n            theme_manager: ThemeManager::new(),\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher: WorkerLauncher::new(),\n            worker_runtime: tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                .enable_all()\n                .build()\n                .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\"),\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            config_rx: None,\n            forge_config: ForgeConfig::default(),\n            data_poll_interval: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            sessions_panel:
